rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 15, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Dana Companies LLC¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant sustained its burden. Plaintiffs presented evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email tocontestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CARROLL WILLIAM PEIL et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 15, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2. Defendant Mayta & Jensen¿s motion for summary judgment or alternatively summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: LINE 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion for summary adjudication of Kaiser Foundation Hospitals¿ affirmative defenses is granted as to defendant¿s 1st through 3rd, 5th through 16th, and 19th through 25th affirmative defenses and denied as to defendant¿s 4th, 17th, 18th and 26th affirmative defenses. As to the 1st through 3rd, 5th through 16th, and 19th through 25th affirmative defenses, plaintiffs sustained their initial burden and defendant failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether any of those affirmative defenses apply. As to the 4th, 17th, 18th and 26th affirmative defenses, plaintiffs sustained their initial burden and plaintiffs¿ complaint and responses to defendant¿s contention discovery create a triable issue whether those affirmative defenses apply. Although unverified, plaintiffs¿ complaint may be considered. Plaintiffs¿ objections to its responses to defendants¿ special discovery are overruled. Defendant¿s opposition was timely and was considered by the court. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to B & P Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312

rulings.Case Title: LEWIS WISECARVER VS. AMCORD, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Vicki Nunes As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, AUGUST 15, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 4. Plaintiff¿s motion to appoint Vicki Nunes as successor-in-interest to and substitute successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
